title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: For questions 9 - 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The Amazon rainforest is the largest tropical forest on Earth, (0) which covers approximately five and a half million square kilometres across nine South American nations. It has long been recognised as one of the planet's most critical ecological systems. (9) Although a significant proportion of its plant and animal species remain unstudied by science, researchers have already documented an extraordinary range of biodiversity within its boundaries. (10) Such is the complexity of Amazonian ecosystems that the loss of a single keystone species can trigger cascading effects throughout an entire food web. The forest plays a fundamental role in regulating global climate, and (11) Provided that it retains sufficient tree cover, it continues to absorb and store vast quantities of atmospheric carbon dioxide. Not (12) only does it produce around one fifth of the world's freshwater through its rainfall cycle, but it also drives weather patterns across a significant portion of the South American continent. (13) Regardless of the precise rate at which deforestation is occurring, its cumulative effects on biodiversity and the global carbon cycle are a matter of serious scientific concern. Not (14) until international agreements began to assign an economic value to standing forests did mainstream financial institutions start to account for forest loss in their risk assessments. (15) Before European colonisation, indigenous peoples had inhabited and sustainably managed Amazonian forests for thousands of years. Their knowledge of plant species and ecological relationships represents an irreplaceable cultural and scientific resource. (16) Whether the Amazon's future is shaped primarily by conservation policy or by continued economic development, the decisions made in the coming decades will have consequences extending far beyond the region itself.

example.text: The Amazon rainforest is the largest tropical forest on Earth, (0) which covers approximately five and a half million square kilometres across nine South American nations.

questions.explanation: Concessive conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession between two clauses.
Emphatic determiner in the inverted structure 'Such was/is the [noun] that...' to express a high degree.
Conditional conjunction meaning 'on the condition that', used to introduce a condition and its result.
Part of the correlative conjunction 'Not only... but also', which requires subject-auxiliary inversion after 'Not only'.
Part of the fixed prepositional phrase 'Regardless of', meaning 'without being affected by'.
Part of the 'Not until' negative fronting structure, which requires subject-auxiliary inversion.
Temporal conjunction introducing a point in time that preceded the events described in the main clause.
Conjunction introducing two alternatives or possibilities in a summarising conclusion.
